scenarios.title: Order Service OOM Crash
scenarios.incident_summary: Multiple 5xx errors reported on /api/orders. The order-svc appears unresponsive and health checks are failing. Customer-facing impact confirmed.

ground_truth.root_cause: Out of memory error in order-svc due to a memory leak in the request handler causing the JVM to exceed its heap limit and crash repeatedly.

error_patterns: GC overhead limit exceeded — pausing all threads
Failed to allocate 256MB for new buffer pool
warn_patterns: Heap usage at 92% — approaching configured limit

error_patterns: Upstream service order-svc returned 502 Bad Gateway
Circuit breaker OPEN for upstream order-svc
warn_patterns: Upstream order-svc response time 12000ms exceeds 5000ms threshold

alert_config.title: Service order-svc Not Responding
alert_config.description: Health check failed for order-svc. Last 5 probes timed out. Service appears down.

alert_config.title: High Error Rate on API Gateway
alert_config.description: Error rate for /api/orders endpoint exceeded 15% threshold. Currently at 28%.

scenarios.title: Order Database Disk Full
scenarios.incident_summary: Order creation failing with database write errors. Customers unable to complete purchases. The order-db service is reporting critical storage issues.

ground_truth.root_cause: Order database disk volume reached 100% capacity due to unarchived transaction logs and unbounded query audit logging filling the disk.

error_patterns: ERROR: could not write to file 'pg_wal/000000010000000100000042': No space left on device
PANIC: could not write to log file: No space left on device
FATAL: database system is in recovery mode — refusing connections
ERROR: could not extend file 'base/16384/2619': No space left on device
warn_patterns: WARNING: disk usage at 98% — approaching critical threshold
WARNING: WAL archiving falling behind — 12 segments pending
WARNING: autovacuum cancelled due to insufficient disk space

error_patterns: Failed to insert order record — database write error
Upstream order-svc returned 500 Internal Server Error

alert_config.title: Disk Usage Critical on order-db
alert_config.description: Disk utilization at 100%. Database unable to perform write operations. Immediate intervention required.

alert_config.title: Order Service Elevated Error Rate
alert_config.description: Error rate for order-svc exceeded 40%. All write operations failing.

scenarios.title: Auth Service OOM Kill
scenarios.incident_summary: Users unable to log in or authenticate API requests. The auth-svc is not responding to health checks. Other services appear functional but returning 401 errors.

ground_truth.root_cause: Auth service process was killed by the OOM killer after a memory leak in the JWT token cache caused resident memory to exceed the 4GB container limit.

error_patterns: FATAL: process killed (OOM) — container memory limit 4096MB exceeded
Service terminated with signal SIGKILL (exit code 137)
Failed to allocate memory for JWT token cache expansion
warn_patterns: Heap usage at 95% — GC unable to free sufficient memory
Token cache size: 2.1M entries — unbounded growth detected
GC pause time: 6200ms — application threads frozen

error_patterns: Upstream auth-svc returned 502 Bad Gateway — health check failed
Authentication failed — auth service unreachable, returning 401
warn_patterns: Auth service response time 15000ms exceeds 3000ms timeout
Falling back to cached auth tokens (stale TTL: 300s)

alert_config.title: Service auth-svc Not Responding
alert_config.description: Health check failed for auth-svc. Process appears to have been OOM killed. Container restart pending.

alert_config.title: Authentication Failures Elevated
alert_config.description: 401 response rate increased to 35%. Auth service appears unreachable.
